sglang.title: Increase the number of thread limitation for tp worker managers.
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Increase the number of thread limitation for tp worker managers.
Improve MMLU benchmark scripts
Tune block sizes for fp8 fused_moe kernels
Support sentencepiece tokenizer

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix embedding to support 2D inputs
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes the VocabParallelEmbedding layer to support 2D input shapes (i.e., [batch_size, seq_len]) as well as 1D.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][TPU] Fix KV cache size calculation
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently, the KV cache size for the TPU backend is calculated incorrectly. This PR fixes it.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Adding bias epilogue support for `cutlass_scaled_mm`
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for a bias term using the new epilogue infrastructure from #5391. This is a part of the AQ kernel push (#3975) we're working on at Neural Magic. In future PRs, we will add support for asymmetric activation quantization by folding the activation zero-point into the bias.
BEFORE S

vllm.title: [Frontend] Add tokenize/detokenize endpoints
vllm.author: sasha0552
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
OpenAI has one tokenizer for all its models, which can be used with the tiktoken library, but for everyone else, it is very difficult to determine which tokenizer to use.
This PR adds tokenize/detokenize endpoints for token counting or other uses of tokens in end applications.
Example (Llama 3):
$ c

vllm.title: [Hardware][TPU] Support parallel sampling & Swapping
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds parallel sampling and swapping support for the TPU backend.
NOTE: Swapping is not implemented for the TPU backend yet. Therefore, vLLM will raise an error if OOM happens during parallel sampling.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][TPU] Fix CPU cache allocation
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes the CPU cache allocation for the TPU backend. Also, it avoids using dynamo for the swapping op.

vllm.title: Support CPU inference with VSX PowerPC ISA
vllm.author: ChipKerchner
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Support CPU inference with VSX PowerPC ISA.
This PR provides basic CPU support for a PowerPC (Power9 & POWER10).  Additional PRs in the future will address further performance.
To build:
docker build --format docker -t vllm:v0.5.0-cpu -f Dockerfile.ppc64le .

Run the api server with:
docker run -ti

vllm.title: [doc] update usage of env var to avoid conflict
vllm.author: youkaichao

vllm.title: [Misc] Add example for LLaVA-NeXT
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There has been some user confusion (e.g, #1751 (comment), #4194 (comment), #4199 (comment)) on how to serve and use LLaVA-NexT on vLLM even though the support for it is currently experimental.
This PR adds an example with a note on its current limitation.


 PR Checklist (Click to Expand) 
Thank you

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix cuda graph for MLPSpeculator
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Just applied this adjustment from the Medusa PR #4978 (thanks @abhigoyal1997!)

vllm.title: [Doc] Add note about context length in Phi-3-Vision example
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
To avoid user confusion like in #5883, this PR adds a comment to the example asking users to use a shorter context length if OOM occurs.
FIX #5883
